surface_verification.justification: The user-facing surface this iterate adds is the record_ci_supplychain_ack CLI. It is driven end-to-end through rec.main([...]) against a real git worktree, then verified by the F11 gate reading the committed tree — the same working-tree -> commit boundary a real iterate crosses. No web/UI surface exists in this diff.

behaviors.behavior: Detector fires on .github/workflows/**, dependabot.y(a)ml, .github/actions/** and renovate configs; near-misses (docs/.github/..., .github/workflow/, .github/dependabot.json) do not; deletions and renames trigger
Gate FAILS when a CI file is touched with no acknowledgement
Gate PASSES with a valid run- and content-bound acknowledgement
An acknowledgement from a different run is rejected (stale-ack false-green found by external review)
An acknowledgement whose fingerprint covers a different CI path set is rejected (acking a workflow tweak cannot license a dependabot.yml revival)
Editing a CI file AFTER recording invalidates the acknowledgement (content-blindness found by code review)
Filler cannot satisfy the gate: empty/short statement, reference-free consistent_with
A corrupt or non-object results file is reported explicitly, never as 'missing acknowledgement'
Outside a git repo the gate SKIPs; inside a repo an absent --commit fails CLOSED (the one-flag bypass found by code review)
git-quoted non-ASCII paths are still detected (the `^` anchor is not defeated by a leading quote)
Verifier pattern copy == SSoT in BOTH directions, and both implementations agree across a 13-path matrix
Writer CLI produces an acknowledgement the gate accepts across the working-tree -> committed-tree boundary, preserves sibling keys (coverage.total), and refuses when no CI file is touched
SKILL.md keeps the explicit 'never read as pin everything' caveat and never instructs SHA-pinning
